# UC-13.6.1 · Grafana Dashboard Load Time P95 Trending

## Description

Slow dashboard loads frustrate incident responders and hide behind average metrics when a few heavy clients dominate. Tracking p95 per Grafana instance catches database pressure, plugin overhead, and auth latency regressions early.

## Value

Keeps operational and executive dashboards usable during incidents when seconds matter.

## Implementation

Prefer structured JSON access logs; if only text, `rex` latency from nginx-style fields. Exclude asset paths (`public/build`). Split by `org_id` if present. Correlate with Postgres slow-query logs.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:log"
| search "/api/dashboards" OR "/d/"
| eval ms=coalesce(duration_ms, request_duration_ms, response_time_ms)
| where isnotnull(ms)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats perc95(ms) as p95_load_ms, count as hits by _time, host
| eventstats median(p95_load_ms) as baseline by host
| where p95_load_ms > baseline * 1.4 AND hits > 30
| sort -p95_load_ms
```

## Visualization

Line chart (p95 ms), histogram of latency, top dashboards by total time (sum of ms).

## References

- [Grafana — Configure log level](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-grafana/#log)
